stimulus.content: By the 1770s, many American colonists were unhappy with British rule. They felt that the British Parliament was making unfair decisions about the colonies without asking the colonists for their opinions.

stem: Which TWO events helped cause the American Revolution?

choices.text: The Stamp Act placed taxes on printed materials in the colonies
The Louisiana Purchase doubled the size of the United States
British soldiers fired on colonists at the Boston Massacre
Lewis and Clark explored the western territories

answer_explanation: The Stamp Act (1765) angered colonists by taxing printed goods without colonial representation. The Boston Massacre (1770) increased anti-British feelings when soldiers killed five colonists. The Louisiana Purchase and Lewis and Clark expedition happened after the Revolution.
